_doc: Shared RUST qualname conformance corpus (Loomweave <-> Wardline). Mirrors tests/conformance/qualnames.json (the Python corpus) but for the Rust dialect. For Rust, LOOMWEAVE IS AUTHORITATIVE: the dialect is fixed by ADR-049 (docs/loomweave/adr/ADR-049-rust-qualname-canonicalization.md) and these 'expected' values were GENERATED by running the loomweave-plugin-rust extractor (extract_file / module_path_for), never hand-authored. Loomweave hosts this file at fixtures/qualnames_rust.json; Wardline vendors a pinned copy to tests/conformance/qualnames_rust.json and must reproduce 'expected' BYTE-FOR-BYTE from its tree-sitter frontend. Inverts the Python arrangement (Wardline seeded qualnames.json; here Loomweave seeds) because Loomweave's whole-tree extractor is the oracle that defines the Rust locator. The parity test crates/loomweave-plugin-rust/tests/qualname_conformance.rs fails loudly on any divergence: fix the extractor or this fixture, never silently. Nothing imports this file at runtime.

_dialect_summary.crate_rooted: first segment is the crate name, '-' normalised to '_' (loomweave-cli -> loomweave_cli); discovered from Cargo.toml [package].name read as TEXT, longest-path-prefix match (crate_roots.rs). Falls back to the dir holding src/lib.rs|src/main.rs.
_dialect_summary.module_route: crate root + mod tree to the item; lib.rs/main.rs/mod.rs contribute NO segment; inline `mod foo {}` nests. #[path] is NOT yet honoured (KNOWN-GAP, sp2).
_dialect_summary.free_items: <crate>.<mods>.<name> (function, struct; enum/trait/const/static/type_alias/macro are Phase 1b, not yet emitted).
_dialect_summary.trait_impl_method: <Type>.impl[<Trait-last-segment-with-concrete-generic-args>].<method>  e.g. Foo.impl[Display].fmt, Foo.impl[From<i32>].from. Lifetimes dropped; concrete type/const generic args KEPT (they are part of the key).
_dialect_summary.inherent_impl_method: <Type>.impl#<positional-generics>#<ordinal>.<method>  e.g. Foo.impl#<>#0.bar, Foo.impl#<$0>#0.get. Generic params rendered POSITIONALLY ($0,$1,... De Bruijn) so a param rename does not churn. The ordinal is source-order among inherent impls of the SAME self-type WITHIN ONE ITEM LIST (module scope); it RESETS inside a nested `mod`.
_dialect_summary.cfg_twins: an item whose bare path collides with a cfg-gated sibling (all cfg variants are visible) gets a normalised @cfg(<pred>) suffix: f@cfg(unix). Predicate whitespace-stripped, any()/all() args sorted. A unique-path item gets NO suffix.
_dialect_summary.async: async fn renders IDENTICALLY to a sync fn (no suffix).
_dialect_summary.kind: the entity-ID kind segment is 'function' for EVERY Rust callable (free fn, inherent method, associated fn, trait method) and 'struct'/'module' for those. There is NO 'method' id-kind. A semantic function/method distinction, if a consumer needs it, must ride Entity metadata, not the locator.
_dialect_summary.closures_and_nested_fns: NOT emitted as entities. The extractor never descends into function bodies, so closures and nested `fn` items have no locator. A finding inside one must be attributed to the nearest enclosing named item. This deliberately avoids the positional {closure#N} churn that would destabilise the SEI locator.
_dialect_summary.macros: macro-generated items are NOT indexed (syn does not expand; tree-sitter cannot see expansion). Both engines agree the generated items do not exist. The macro_rules! definition itself is Phase 1b.
_dialect_summary.reproducibility_tiers: Each case carries 'reproducibility': 'slice-1' = the qualname SUFFIX (everything the single file's syntax determines: impl disc, cfg, within-file ordinal, generics, locals-folding) is reproducible by a one-file tree-sitter pass NOW. 'sp2' = needs Loomweave's whole-tree view (crate-name-from-Cargo.toml, cross-file module route, #[path], cross-file inherent-impl ordinals). The CRATE-ROOT PREFIX of every entity is itself sp2 until Wardline reads manifests; slice-1 cases are reproducible modulo that shared prefix caveat.

_consumer_comparison: HOW A SECOND PRODUCER (e.g. Wardline) COMPARES AGAINST THIS CORPUS — read before vendoring. The `entities[].expected` list is Loomweave's FULL emission in source order: it includes `module` entities (the file-scope module + every nested inline `mod`) and its `kind` field is the entity-ID kind segment, which is `function` for EVERY callable (free fn, method, assoc fn) — there is no semantic `method` kind. A finding-producer that emits only function/method entities (as Wardline's discover_file_entities does — modules are scope-only, and §6.2 tags impl fns `kind=method`) MUST NOT compare with raw list-equality against `expected`, or it will fail on rows it never emits. The contract is: (1) the BYTE-EXACT obligation is the `qualname` of every NON-`module` row — that is the string folded into the fingerprint, and it must match character-for-character; (2) `kind` is informational here (the locator id-kind), NOT a semantic tag — Wardline's semantic `method` corresponds to this corpus's id-kind `function` for impl-scoped fns (the documented kind boundary, analogous to the `None <-> ""` module boundary in loomweave_qualname_parity.json), so either map `method->function` or compare qualname-only; (3) `module` rows are validated against the `module_route` section (the path->module routing), NOT re-emitted as entities. So the recommended Wardline parity test: extract the file, take your function/method entities, and assert each produces a `qualname` present in this case's non-`module` `expected` qualnames. Do not edit the vendored copy to drop rows — apply this comparison rule instead.

module_route.name: lib_root
module_route.note: lib.rs contributes no segment; module IS the crate root.

module_route.name: mod_rs_collapse
module_route.note: mod.rs contributes no segment; collapses to its directory.

module_route.name: path_attr_known_gap
module_route.note: If `renamed.rs` is mounted via `#[path="renamed.rs"] mod logical;` its logical module is `demo.logical`, but module_path_for routes purely by file path and emits `demo.renamed`. #[path] is NOT yet honoured (ADR-049 lists it; module_path.rs does not implement it). This row pins what the extractor ACTUALLY emits today; both engines must match the emitted form, and #[path]-correct routing is a shared sp2 task.

entities.name: free_fns_and_struct
entities.source:
pub struct Widget { a: i32 }
pub fn helper(x: i32) -> bool { x > 0 }

entities.name: inherent_method
entities.source:
struct Foo;
impl Foo { fn bar(&self) {} }

entities.name: trait_method
entities.source:
struct Foo;
impl std::fmt::Display for Foo { fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter) -> std::fmt::Result { Ok(()) } }

entities.name: trait_method_collision
entities.note: Display::fmt and Debug::fmt on the same type stay distinct because the method carries its impl's trait discriminator.

entities.name: multiple_inherent_ordinal
entities.note: Two inherent impls of one type in one file: source-order ordinal #0 then #1.

entities.name: generic_trait_impl_concrete_args
entities.note: Concrete trait generic args are part of the key: From<i32> and From<u32> are distinct impls.

entities.name: positional_generic_param
entities.note: Inherent-impl generic params render positionally ($0). See positional_generic_param_renamed for the rename-stability pair: identical expected qualname proves a <T>-><U> rename does not churn.
entities.source:
struct Foo<T>(T);
impl<T> Foo<T> { fn get(&self) {} }

entities.name: positional_generic_param_renamed
entities.note: Same as positional_generic_param with T renamed to U; expected qualname is byte-identical (positional De Bruijn rendering), pinning rename-stability at the corpus level.
entities.source:
struct Foo<U>(U);
impl<U> Foo<U> { fn get(&self) {} }

entities.name: cfg_twin
entities.note: Two same-path fns gated on mutually-exclusive cfgs; each gets a normalised @cfg() suffix. All cfg variants are visible (spec §5).

entities.name: struct_cfg_twin
entities.note: cfg-twin discrimination is ITEM-general, not functions-only (ADR-049 §3): a struct gated on mutually-exclusive cfgs gets the @cfg suffix too. Without it both variants emit `demo.m.S` and the writer's ON CONFLICT silently drops one. The same holds for inline `mod` twins. Counted per-kind, so `fn S` and `struct S` do not interfere.

entities.name: async_fn_renders_identically
entities.note: async fn carries no suffix; afn and sfn differ only by name. Wardline's index walk must not skip the async modifier node.
entities.source:
pub async fn afn() {}
pub fn sfn() {}

entities.name: nested_fn_is_not_an_entity
entities.note: DIVERGENCE FROM WARDLINE'S PROPOSAL: `fn inner` nested in `fn outer` is NOT emitted (no demo.m.outer.<locals>.inner). The extractor never descends into bodies. A finding in `inner` attributes to demo.m.outer.

entities.name: closure_is_not_an_entity
entities.note: DIVERGENCE FROM WARDLINE'S PROPOSAL: a closure is NOT emitted (no demo.m.f.<locals>.{closure#0}). Kills the positional churn problem. A finding in the closure attributes to demo.m.f.

entities.name: nested_inline_mod
entities.note: Inline `mod inner {}` produces a nested module entity AND prefixes its items. (Crate-prefix `demo` is the shared sp2 caveat; the inline-mod nesting itself is single-file.)

entities.name: ordinal_resets_per_module_scope
entities.note: The inherent-impl ordinal is scoped PER ITEM LIST: top-level Foo gets #0,#1; the `Foo` inside `mod inner` resets to #0. Wardline must scope its counter per module, not file-global.

entities.name: macro_invocation_generates_no_entity
entities.note: AGREEMENT: a macro_rules! definition + invocation generates a `generated` fn at compile time, but neither engine sees it (syn does not expand; tree-sitter cannot). Only the file-scope module is emitted. Both engines must agree the generated item does not exist.
